event: TSMC founded by Morris Chang in Hsinchu Science Park
notes: World's first dedicated semiconductor foundry; initial capital TWD 5.5 billion; Philips 27.5% stake

event: TSMC IPO on Taiwan Stock Exchange
notes: TSMC listed on TWSE; later ADR on NYSE in 1997
UMC restructured as pure-play foundry; became TSMC's primary competitor in Taiwan

event: TSMC reaches 180nm process node mass production
notes: First advanced node; enabled early mobile and computing chips
Copper interconnect technology adopted for improved performance
Strained silicon technology introduction
Immersion lithography adopted; major capacity expansion in Tainan
Low-k dielectric and immersion lithography refinement
Major smartphone chip node; Apple relationship deepens
Hugely successful node; longest-running profitable node in foundry history; HKMG technology
Transition node with limited adoption; bridge to FinFET

event: ASML ships first production EUV lithography system (NXE:3300B)
notes: First commercially available EUV scanner; years behind schedule but transformative technology
First FinFET node; won Apple A9 chip orders over Samsung
Enabled Apple A11 Bionic; TSMC surpasses Intel in transistor density
Samsung Foundry established as independent business unit to compete with TSMC
First EUV-capable node (7nm+ used EUV); Apple A12 and AMD Zen 2 key customers
First process using EUV lithography in volume production at TSMC
$12 billion initial investment for 5nm fab in Phoenix; later expanded significantly
Apple A14 Bionic first customer; 1.8x logic density improvement over 7nm

event: US Commerce Dept Huawei chip ban takes full effect
notes: Banned TSMC and all foundries using US equipment from supplying Huawei; massive geopolitical impact

event: Global chip shortage reaches peak intensity
notes: Auto industry and consumer electronics severely impacted; highlighted TSMC strategic importance
Joint venture with Sony and Denso; 22/28nm and 12/16nm capacity; $8.6 billion investment
Apple M2 Pro/Max and A17 Pro first customers; GAA-like FinFlex architecture

event: US CHIPS and Science Act signed into law
notes: $52.7 billion for semiconductor manufacturing and R&D; $39 billion in manufacturing incentives

event: TSMC Arizona first tool-in ceremony with President Biden
notes: TSMC expanded Arizona commitment to $40 billion for two fabs (4nm and 3nm)
43 billion euro package to double EU semiconductor market share to 20% by 2030

event: Japan CHIPS Act funding approved (revised semiconductor strategy)
notes: 2 trillion yen ($13.4 billion) subsidy package for domestic semiconductor production

event: TSMC Kumamoto (JASM) fab construction progresses on schedule
notes: Expected production start in Q1 2024; fastest fab construction in TSMC history
Joint venture with Bosch NXP and Infineon; 28/22nm and 16/12nm; 10 billion euro investment

event: SMIC achieves 7nm production for Huawei Kirin 9000S
notes: Huawei Mate 60 Pro contained SMIC N+2 7nm chip; raised concerns about export control effectiveness
First TSMC fab in Japan operational; production of 22/28nm chips for automotive and industrial

event: TSMC receives $6.6 billion US CHIPS Act award for Arizona fabs
notes: Plus $5 billion in loans; for three Arizona fabs producing 4nm 3nm and 2nm chips

event: Samsung receives $6.4 billion US CHIPS Act award for Taylor Texas fab
notes: For advanced logic fab in Taylor TX plus expansion in Austin; 2nm and below target

event: Intel receives $8.5 billion US CHIPS Act award
notes: Largest CHIPS Act award; for fabs in Arizona Ohio New Mexico and Oregon

event: TSMC begins N3E enhanced 3nm mass production
notes: Improved yield and performance variant of 3nm; broad customer adoption

event: TSMC Arizona Fab 21 Phase 1 begins pilot production
notes: First advanced-node semiconductor production in US; 4nm process technology

event: ASML ships first High-NA EUV system (TWINSCAN EXE:5000)
notes: 0.55 NA EUV system for 2nm and below; shipped to Intel; $380 million per unit

event: TSMC N2 (2nm) nanosheet process enters risk production
notes: Gate-all-around nanosheet transistor architecture; 10-15% speed improvement over N3E

event: TSMC Kumamoto second fab (JASM Phase 2) construction underway
notes: 6/12nm process technology planned; total investment over $20 billion with Japanese government subsidies
European semiconductor sovereignty initiative; expected production in 2027
